papers.title: Tstars-Tryon 1.0: Robust and Realistic Virtual Try-On for Diverse Fashion Items
papers.abstract: Abstract A commercial-scale virtual try-on system achieves high success rates, photorealistic results, and real-time performance through integrated system design and multi-stage training.  AI-generated summary Recent advances in image generation and editing have opened new opportunities for virtual try-on. However, existing methods still struggle to meet complex real-world demands. We present Tstars-Tryon 1.0, a commercial-scale virtual try-on system that is robust, realistic, versatile, and highly efficient. First, our system maintains a high success rate across challenging cases like extreme poses, severe illumination variations, motion blur, and other in-the-wild conditions. Second, it delivers highly photorealistic results with fine-grained details, faithfully preserving garment texture, material properties, and structural characteristics, while largely avoiding common AI-generated artifacts. Third, beyond apparel try-on, our model supports flexible multi-image composition (up to 6 reference images) across 8 fashion categories, with coordinated control over person identity and background. Fourth, to overcome the latency bottlenecks of commercial deployment, our system is heavily optimized for inference speed, delivering near real-time generation for a seamless user experience. These capabilities are enabled by an integrated system design spanning end-to-end model architecture, a scalable data engine, robust infrastructure, and a multi-stage training paradigm. Extensive evaluation and large-scale product deployment demonstrate that Tstars-Tryon1.0 achieves leading overall performance. To support future research, we also release a comprehensive benchmark. The model has been deployed at an industrial scale on the Taobao App, serving millions of users with tens of millions of requests.

ru.title: Фотореалистичная примерка одежды в реальном времени
ru.desc: Tstars-Tryon 1.0 — это коммерческая система виртуальной примерки одежды, которая использует современные методы генеративного моделирования для создания фотореалистичных результатов. Система достигает высокого процента успеха даже в сложных условиях съёмки (экстремальные позы, резкие изменения освещения, размытие движения) благодаря интегрированной архитектуре модели и масштабируемому конвейеру подготовки данных. Модель поддерживает гибкую композицию с использованием нескольких эталонных изображений (до 6) из 8 категорий моды и позволяет независимо контролировать идентичность персоны и фон. Система оптимизирована для высокой скорости инференса и работает в реальном времени, что позволило развернуть её в масштабах индустрии в приложении Taobao для обслуживания миллионов пользователей.

en.title: Revolutionizing Virtual Try-Ons with Tstars-Tryon 1.0
en.desc: The paper presents Tstars-Tryon 1.0, a cutting-edge virtual try-on system that excels in real-world applications. It achieves high success rates even in challenging conditions like extreme poses and varying lighting, while producing photorealistic images that accurately reflect garment details. The system is designed for efficiency, allowing for near real-time performance and supporting multiple images across various fashion categories. Its robust architecture and multi-stage training approach ensure it meets the demands of commercial deployment, as evidenced by its successful integration into the Taobao App.

zh.title: 虚拟试衣的未来：高效、真实、灵活
zh.desc: 本文介绍了一种商业规模的虚拟试衣系统Tstars-Tryon 1.0，该系统通过集成设计和多阶段训练实现了高成功率、逼真的效果和实时性能。该系统能够在极端姿势、光照变化和运动模糊等复杂情况下保持高成功率，并生成细致入微的逼真图像，忠实保留服装的纹理和材料特性。除了服装试穿外，该模型还支持多图像组合，能够灵活处理多达6张参考图像，并协调控制人物身份和背景。为了克服商业部署中的延迟瓶颈，该系统经过优化，能够实现近实时生成，提供无缝的用户体验。

papers.title: CoInteract: Physically-Consistent Human-Object Interaction Video Synthesis via Spatially-Structured Co-Generation
papers.abstract: Abstract CoInteract presents an end-to-end framework for human-object interaction video synthesis using a Diffusion Transformer backbone with specialized modules for structural stability and physical plausibility.  AI-generated summary Synthesizing human--object interaction (HOI) videos has broad practical value in e-commerce, digital advertising, and virtual marketing. However, current diffusion models, despite their photorealistic rendering capability, still frequently fail on (i) the structural stability of sensitive regions such as hands and faces and (ii) physically plausible contact (e.g., avoiding hand--object interpenetration). We present CoInteract, an end-to-end framework for HOI video synthesis conditioned on a person reference image, a product reference image, text prompts, and speech audio. CoInteract introduces two complementary designs embedded into a Diffusion Transformer (DiT) backbone. First, we propose a Human-Aware Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) that routes tokens to lightweight, region-specialized experts via spatially supervised routing, improving fine-grained structural fidelity with minimal parameter overhead. Second, we propose Spatially-Structured Co-Generation, a dual-stream training paradigm that jointly models an RGB appearance stream and an auxiliary HOI structure stream to inject interaction geometry priors. During training, the HOI stream attends to RGB tokens and its supervision regularizes shared backbone weights; at inference, the HOI branch is removed for zero-overhead RGB generation. Experimental results demonstrate that CoInteract significantly outperforms existing methods in structural stability, logical consistency, and interaction realism.

ru.title: Реалистичный синтез видео взаимодействия человека с объектом через структурированную генерацию
ru.desc: CoInteract — это фреймворк для синтеза видео взаимодействия человека с объектом, построенный на основе Diffusion Transformer. Система использует специализированный механизм Mixture-of-Experts с пространственной маршрутизацией для повышения точности воспроизведения чувствительных областей, таких как руки и лицо. Авторы предлагают двухпоточную парадигму обучения, которая совместно моделирует RGB-поток и вспомогательный поток структуры взаимодействия, чтобы внедрить приоры геометрии контакта. Метод демонстрирует значительное улучшение в структурной стабильности, логической согласованности и реалистичности взаимодействия человека с предметом.

en.title: Revolutionizing Human-Object Interaction Video Synthesis
en.desc: CoInteract is a novel framework designed for synthesizing videos that depict human-object interactions (HOI) using a Diffusion Transformer architecture. It addresses common challenges in video generation, such as maintaining structural stability in sensitive areas like hands and faces, and ensuring physically plausible interactions without object interpenetration. The framework incorporates a Human-Aware Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) for improved detail and a dual-stream training approach that models both appearance and interaction structure. Experimental results show that CoInteract achieves superior performance in terms of realism and consistency compared to existing methods.

zh.title: 人机交互视频合成的新突破
zh.desc: CoInteract是一个用于人机交互视频合成的端到端框架，采用扩散变换器作为基础架构，并引入了专门的模块以确保结构稳定性和物理合理性。该框架能够根据人物参考图像、产品参考图像、文本提示和语音音频生成高质量的HOI视频。CoInteract的两个创新设计包括人类感知的专家混合模型和空间结构共同生成，前者通过空间监督路由将令牌分配给轻量级的区域专家，后者则通过双流训练模式共同建模RGB外观流和辅助HOI结构流。实验结果表明，CoInteract在结构稳定性、逻辑一致性和交互真实感方面显著优于现有方法。

papers.title: AgentSPEX: An Agent SPecification and EXecution Language
papers.abstract: Abstract AgentSPEX is a domain-specific language and framework for creating structured, modular, and interpretable large language model agent workflows with explicit control flow and state management.  AI-generated summary Language-model agent systems commonly rely on reactive prompting, in which a single instruction guides the model through an open-ended sequence of reasoning and tool-use steps, leaving control flow and intermediate state implicit and making agent behavior potentially difficult to control. Orchestration frameworks such as LangGraph, DSPy, and CrewAI impose greater structure through explicit workflow definitions, but tightly couple workflow logic with Python, making agents difficult to maintain and modify. In this paper, we introduce AgentSPEX, an Agent SPecification and EXecution Language for specifying LLM-agent workflows with explicit control flow and modular structure, along with a customizable agent harness. AgentSPEX supports typed steps, branching and loops, parallel execution, reusable submodules, and explicit state management, and these workflows execute within an agent harness that provides tool access, a sandboxed virtual environment, and support for checkpointing, verification, and logging. Furthermore, we provide a visual editor with synchronized graph and workflow views for authoring and inspection. We include ready-to-use agents for deep research and scientific research, and we evaluate AgentSPEX on 7 benchmarks. Finally, we show through a user study that AgentSPEX provides a more interpretable and accessible workflow-authoring paradigm than a popular existing agent framework.

ru.title: Структурированный язык для явного управления рабочими процессами LLM-агентов
ru.desc: AgentSPEX — это специализированный язык и фреймворк для разработки рабочих процессов на основе больших языковых моделей с явным управлением потоком выполнения и состоянием. В отличие от реактивного подхода, где модель следует единой инструкции через неявные шаги рассуждения и использования инструментов, AgentSPEX обеспечивает структурированность через явное определение рабочих процессов с поддержкой типизированных шагов, ветвлений, циклов и параллельного выполнения. Фреймворк включает интерпретируемую агентную архитектуру с явным управлением состоянием, запись в журнал, контрольные точки и визуальный редактор для проектирования и отладки. Исследование показало, что AgentSPEX обеспечивает более простоту и интерпретируемость при разработке агентов по сравнению с существующими фреймворками.

en.title: Empowering LLM Agents with Structured Workflows
en.desc: AgentSPEX is a specialized language and framework designed to create structured workflows for large language model (LLM) agents, emphasizing clear control flow and state management. Unlike traditional reactive prompting methods that can lead to unpredictable agent behavior, AgentSPEX allows for explicit definitions of workflows, making them easier to understand and modify. It supports features like typed steps, branching, loops, and reusable modules, all while providing a customizable environment for executing these workflows. The framework also includes a visual editor for better workflow authoring and inspection, and it has been shown to enhance interpretability compared to existing frameworks through user studies.

zh.title: AgentSPEX：可解释的代理工作流框架
zh.desc: AgentSPEX 是一种专门用于创建结构化、模块化和可解释的大型语言模型代理工作流的语言和框架。与传统的反应式提示方法不同，AgentSPEX 通过明确的控制流和状态管理，使得代理的行为更易于控制。它支持类型化步骤、分支和循环、并行执行以及可重用的子模块，提供了一个可定制的代理环境。通过可视化编辑器，用户可以更方便地创建和检查工作流，提升了工作流的可解释性和可访问性。

papers.title: AnyRecon: Arbitrary-View 3D Reconstruction with Video Diffusion Model
papers.abstract: Abstract AnyRecon enables scalable 3D reconstruction from arbitrary sparse inputs using diffusion models with persistent scene memory and geometry-aware conditioning for improved geometric consistency.  AI-generated summary Sparse-view 3D reconstruction is essential for modeling scenes from casual captures, but remain challenging for non-generative reconstruction. Existing diffusion-based approaches mitigates this issues by synthesizing novel views, but they often condition on only one or two capture frames, which restricts geometric consistency and limits scalability to large or diverse scenes. We propose AnyRecon, a scalable framework for reconstruction from arbitrary and unordered sparse inputs that preserves explicit geometric control while supporting flexible conditioning cardinality. To support long-range conditioning, our method constructs a persistent global scene memory via a prepended capture view cache, and removes temporal compression to maintain frame-level correspondence under large viewpoint changes. Beyond better generative model, we also find that the interplay between generation and reconstruction is crucial for large-scale 3D scenes. Thus, we introduce a geometry-aware conditioning strategy that couples generation and reconstruction through an explicit 3D geometric memory and geometry-driven capture-view retrieval. To ensure efficiency, we combine 4-step diffusion distillation with context-window sparse attention to reduce quadratic complexity. Extensive experiments demonstrate robust and scalable reconstruction across irregular inputs, large viewpoint gaps, and long trajectories.

ru.title: Масштабируемая реконструкция 3D из разреженных данных с геометрическим контролем
ru.desc: AnyRecon — это метод для трёхмерной реконструкции сцен из произвольного разреженного набора изображений с использованием диффузионных моделей. Ключевой инновацией является введение глобальной памяти сцены, которая сохраняет информацию о всех наблюдаемых кадрах без временного сжатия, обеспечивая лучшую согласованность геометрии при больших изменениях ракурса. Авторы предлагают геометрически-осведомлённую стратегию кондиционирования, которая явно используют трёхмерную геометрическую память для улучшения взаимодействия между синтезом и реконструкцией. Метод использует дистилляцию диффузии и разреженное внимание для повышения вычислительной эффективности, что позволяет масштабировать подход на большие и сложные сцены.

en.title: AnyRecon: Scalable 3D Reconstruction with Geometric Consistency
en.desc: AnyRecon is a new method for creating 3D models from sparse and unordered images using advanced diffusion models. It improves the quality of the 3D reconstruction by maintaining a persistent memory of the scene and using geometry-aware techniques to ensure that the generated views are geometrically consistent. This approach allows for better handling of large and diverse scenes by conditioning on multiple input frames rather than just one or two. The method also incorporates efficient processing techniques to manage complexity, making it scalable for various applications in 3D reconstruction.

zh.title: AnyRecon：灵活高效的3D重建新方法
zh.desc: AnyRecon 是一个可扩展的 3D 重建框架，能够从任意稀疏输入中进行重建，使用扩散模型和持久场景记忆来提高几何一致性。该方法通过构建全局场景记忆，支持灵活的条件输入，解决了传统方法在几何一致性和可扩展性方面的限制。我们引入了一种几何感知的条件策略，将生成与重建相结合，以增强大规模 3D 场景的处理能力。实验结果表明，AnyRecon 在处理不规则输入和大视角变化时表现出色，具有良好的可扩展性。

papers.title: TEMPO: Scaling Test-time Training for Large Reasoning Models
papers.abstract: Abstract TEMPO is a test-time training framework that alternates policy refinement with critic recalibration to sustain performance improvements in language models without diversity collapse.  AI-generated summary Test-time training (TTT) adapts model parameters on unlabeled test instances during inference time, which continuously extends capabilities beyond the reach of offline training. Despite initial gains, existing TTT methods for LRMs plateau quickly and do not benefit from additional test-time compute. Without external calibration, the self-generated reward signal increasingly drifts as the policy model evolves, leading to both performance plateaus and diversity collapse. We propose TEMPO, a TTT framework that interleaves policy refinement on unlabeled questions with periodic critic recalibration on a labeled dataset. By formalizing this alternating procedure through the Expectation-Maximization (EM) algorithm, we reveal that prior methods can be interpreted as incomplete variants that omit the crucial recalibration step. Reintroducing this step tightens the evidence lower bound (ELBO) and enables sustained improvement. Across diverse model families (Qwen3 and OLMO3) and reasoning tasks, TEMPO improves OLMO3-7B on AIME 2024 from 33.0% to 51.1% and Qwen3-14B from 42.3% to 65.8%, while maintaining high diversity.
affiliations: The Chinese University of Hong Kong

ru.title: Чередующееся уточнение политики и критика для устойчивого улучшения языковых моделей
ru.desc: TEMPO — это фреймворк обучения во время тестирования, который чередует уточнение политики модели с переоценкой критика для поддержания улучшений в языковых моделях. Авторы показывают, что существующие методы обучения на тесте страдают от смещения самогенерируемого сигнала награды, что приводит к застою производительности и коллапсу разнообразия. Они формализуют процесс чередования политики и критика через алгоритм EM, доказывая, что предыдущие методы были неполными без шага переоценки. На практике TEMPO значительно улучшает результаты моделей Qwen3 и OLMO3 на задачах логического рассуждения, сохраняя при этом разнообразие ответов.

en.title: TEMPO: Sustaining Language Model Performance Through Smart Training
en.desc: TEMPO is a novel test-time training (TTT) framework designed to enhance the performance of language models during inference by refining policies and recalibrating critics. It addresses the issue of performance plateaus and diversity collapse that often occur in existing TTT methods. By alternating between policy refinement on unlabeled data and critic recalibration on labeled data, TEMPO effectively maintains and improves model capabilities. This approach, formalized through the Expectation-Maximization algorithm, demonstrates significant performance gains across various model families and reasoning tasks without sacrificing diversity.

zh.title: TEMPO：提升语言模型性能的新方法
zh.desc: TEMPO是一种测试时训练框架，通过交替进行策略优化和评论者重新校准，来持续提升语言模型的性能而不导致多样性崩溃。测试时训练（TTT）在推理阶段对未标记的测试实例进行模型参数的适应，从而不断扩展模型的能力。现有的TTT方法在初期取得了一定的进展，但很快就会停滞，并且无法利用额外的测试时间计算。TEMPO通过引入评论者重新校准步骤，结合期望最大化（EM）算法，显著提高了模型在多样性和性能上的表现。

papers.title: PlayCoder: Making LLM-Generated GUI Code Playable
papers.abstract: Abstract Large language models struggle to generate logically correct GUI applications, prompting the development of PlayEval benchmark and PlayCoder framework that uses multi-agent approaches to improve functional correctness through iterative repair.  AI-generated summary Large language models (LLMs) have achieved strong results in code generation, but their ability to generate GUI applications, especially games, remains insufficiently studied. Existing benchmarks mainly evaluate correctness through test cases, which are inadequate for GUI applications because these systems are interactive, event-driven, and require correct state transitions across sequences of user actions. Their evaluation therefore should consider interaction flows and UI logic rather than only pass/fail outcomes. To study this problem, we introduce PlayEval, a repository-aware benchmark built from 43 multilingual GUI applications in Python, TypeScript, and JavaScript. Unlike prior GUI benchmarks that are difficult to adapt to desktop environments, PlayEval covers six major GUI application categories and directly supports code-generation evaluation. We further propose Play@k, a metric that measures whether at least one of *k* generated candidates can be played end-to-end without logical errors. To support reliable evaluation, we develop PlayTester, an LLM-based agent that performs task-oriented GUI playthroughs and detects logic violations automatically. Experiments on 10 state-of-the-art code LLMs show that, despite high compilation rates, they achieve near-zero Play@3, revealing major weaknesses in generating logically correct GUI applications. To address this limitation, we present PlayCoder, a multi-agent, repository-aware framework that generates, evaluates, and iteratively repairs GUI application code in a closed loop. PlayCoder substantially improves both functional correctness and semantic alignment for open-source and closed-source models, reaching up to 38.1% Exec@3 and 20.3% Play@3. Case studies further show that it can uncover silent logic bugs missed by traditional metrics and fix them through targeted edits.

ru.title: Многоагентное исправление кода GUI-приложений через итеративное тестирование и ремонт
ru.desc: В статье представлена новая система PlayEval для оценки способности больших языковых моделей генерировать корректные графические приложения и игры, которые требуют правильной обработки последовательностей пользовательских действий и переходов состояний. Авторы разработали PlayTester — агент на основе LLM, который автоматически тестирует интерактивные приложения и обнаруживает логические ошибки, выявив при этом серьёзные проблемы существующих моделей в генерации функционально корректного кода. Для решения этой проблемы предложена система PlayCoder, использующая многоагентный подход для итеративного ремонта сгенерированного кода через автоматическое тестирование и целевые исправления в замкнутом цикле. Экспериментальные результаты показывают, что PlayCoder значительно улучшает функциональную корректность и семантическое соответствие как для открытых, так и для закрытых моделей, достигая показателя Play@3 в 20.3%.

en.title: Enhancing GUI Code Generation with PlayEval and PlayCoder
en.desc: This paper addresses the challenges faced by large language models (LLMs) in generating logically correct graphical user interface (GUI) applications. It introduces PlayEval, a benchmark designed to evaluate GUI applications based on their interactive and event-driven nature, rather than just pass/fail outcomes. The authors propose PlayCoder, a multi-agent framework that iteratively generates and repairs GUI code to enhance functional correctness. Experiments demonstrate that PlayCoder significantly improves the performance of LLMs in generating GUI applications, revealing and fixing logic errors that traditional metrics often miss.

zh.title: 提升GUI应用生成的逻辑正确性
zh.desc: 大型语言模型在生成逻辑正确的图形用户界面（GUI）应用程序方面存在困难，因此开发了PlayEval基准和PlayCoder框架。PlayEval是一个基于多语言GUI应用程序的基准，旨在评估代码生成的功能正确性。PlayCoder则是一个多代理框架，通过迭代修复来提高生成的GUI应用程序的逻辑正确性。实验结果表明，PlayCoder显著改善了开源和闭源模型的功能正确性和语义一致性。

papers.title: ShadowPEFT: Shadow Network for Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning
papers.abstract: Abstract ShadowPEFT is a parameter-efficient fine-tuning framework that performs layer-level refinement through depth-shared shadow modules, offering competitive performance with reduced computational overhead compared to traditional low-rank adaptation methods.  AI-generated summary Parameter-efficient fine-tuning (PEFT) reduces the training cost of full-parameter fine-tuning for large language models (LLMs) by training only a small set of task-specific parameters while freezing the pretrained backbone. However, existing approaches, such as Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA), achieve adaptation by inserting independent low-rank perturbations directly to individual weights, resulting in a local parameterization of adaptation. We propose ShadowPEFT, a centralized PEFT framework that instead performs layer-level refinement through a depth-shared shadow module. At each transformer layer, ShadowPEFT maintains a parallel shadow state and evolves it repeatedly for progressively richer hidden states. This design shifts adaptation from distributed weight-space perturbations to a shared layer-space refinement process. Since the shadow module is decoupled from the backbone, it can be reused across depth, independently pretrained, and optionally deployed in a detached mode, benefiting edge computing scenarios. Experiments on generation and understanding benchmarks show that ShadowPEFT matches or outperforms LoRA and DoRA under comparable trainable-parameter budgets. Additional analyses on shadow pretraining, cross-dataset transfer, parameter scaling, inference latency, and system-level evaluation suggest that centralized layer-space adaptation is a competitive and flexible alternative to conventional low-rank PEFT.

ru.title: Централизованная адаптация на уровне слоёв вместо локальных весовых возмущений
ru.desc: ShadowPEFT — это фреймворк параметрически-эффективной тонкой настройки больших языковых моделей, который использует общие теневые модули на уровне слоёв вместо локальных низкоранговых адаптаций. Вместо того чтобы добавлять независимые возмущения в веса каждого слоя, метод поддерживает параллельное теневое состояние, которое постепенно эволюционирует для создания более богатых скрытых представлений. Благодаря тому что теневой модуль отделён от основной сети, его можно переиспользовать на разной глубине, предварительно обучать отдельно и развёртывать в отключённом режиме, что особенно полезно для вычислений на граничных устройствах. Экспериментальные результаты показывают, что ShadowPEFT конкурирует или превосходит методы LoRA и DoRA при сравнимом бюджете обучаемых параметров.

en.title: Efficient Layer-Level Refinement with ShadowPEFT
en.desc: ShadowPEFT is a new framework for fine-tuning large language models that focuses on efficiency by using a centralized approach. Instead of adjusting individual weights like traditional methods, it refines entire layers through shared shadow modules, which reduces computational costs. This method allows for better performance while maintaining a smaller set of trainable parameters. Experiments show that ShadowPEFT can match or exceed the performance of existing techniques like LoRA, making it a promising option for edge computing applications.

zh.title: ShadowPEFT：高效的层级微调新方法
zh.desc: ShadowPEFT是一种参数高效的微调框架，通过深度共享的影子模块进行层级精细调整，能够在计算开销减少的情况下提供与传统低秩适应方法相媲美的性能。与现有的低秩适应方法不同，ShadowPEFT在每个变换器层中维护一个并行的影子状态，并通过不断演化来获得更丰富的隐藏状态。这种设计将适应过程从分散的权重空间扰动转变为共享的层空间精细调整。实验结果表明，ShadowPEFT在生成和理解基准测试中与LoRA和DoRA相当或更优，展示了其作为传统低秩PEFT的竞争性和灵活性。

papers.title: Chat2Workflow: A Benchmark for Generating Executable Visual Workflows with Natural Language
papers.abstract: Abstract Chat2Workflow presents a benchmark and agentic framework for automating executable visual workflow generation from natural language, revealing significant challenges in achieving industrial-grade automation despite advances in language models.  AI-generated summary At present, executable visual workflows have emerged as a mainstream paradigm in real-world industrial deployments, offering strong reliability and controllability. However, in current practice, such workflows are almost entirely constructed through manual engineering: developers must carefully design workflows, write prompts for each step, and repeatedly revise the logic as requirements evolve-making development costly, time-consuming, and error-prone. To study whether large language models can automate this multi-round interaction process, we introduce Chat2Workflow, a benchmark for generating executable visual workflows directly from natural language, and propose a robust agentic framework to mitigate recurrent execution errors. Chat2Workflow is built from a large collection of real-world business workflows, with each instance designed so that the generated workflow can be transformed and directly deployed to practical workflow platforms such as Dify and Coze. Experimental results show that while state-of-the-art language models can often capture high-level intent, they struggle to generate correct, stable, and executable workflows, especially under complex or changing requirements. Although our agentic framework yields up to 5.34% resolve rate gains, the remaining real-world gap positions Chat2Workflow as a foundation for advancing industrial-grade automation. Code is available at https://github.com/zjunlp/Chat2Workflow.

ru.title: От естественного языка к автоматизации: мостим разрыв между LLM и промышленными рабочими процессами
ru.desc: В статье представлена бенчмарк Chat2Workflow и агентский фреймворк для автоматизации генерации исполняемых визуальных рабочих процессов из естественного языка с использованием больших языковых моделей. Авторы показывают, что хотя современные LLM хорошо понимают высокоуровневые намерения пользователя, они испытывают значительные трудности с генерацией корректных и стабильных рабочих процессов, особенно при сложных или изменяющихся требованиях. Предложенный агентский фреймворк применяет различные стратегии для снижения ошибок при выполнении и достигает увеличения успешности до 5.34%. Исследование подчеркивает существующий разрыв между возможностями AI и требованиями промышленного применения в области автоматизации рабочих процессов.

en.title: Automating Workflows: Bridging Language and Execution
en.desc: Chat2Workflow introduces a new benchmark and framework aimed at automating the creation of executable visual workflows from natural language inputs. Despite advancements in language models, the study highlights significant challenges in achieving reliable automation for industrial applications. The framework addresses the common issues of manual workflow construction, which is often costly and prone to errors. Experimental results indicate that while language models can understand high-level intents, they still struggle with generating accurate and stable workflows, particularly in complex scenarios, making Chat2Workflow a crucial step towards improving automation in real-world settings.

zh.title: 从自然语言到可执行工作流的自动化之路
zh.desc: Chat2Workflow是一个基准和代理框架，旨在从自然语言自动生成可执行的视觉工作流。尽管语言模型取得了进展，但在实现工业级自动化方面仍面临重大挑战。当前的工作流几乎完全依赖手动工程，开发者需要设计工作流、编写每一步的提示，并随着需求变化反复修订逻辑。实验结果表明，尽管最先进的语言模型能够捕捉高层意图，但在生成正确、稳定和可执行的工作流方面仍然存在困难，尤其是在复杂或变化的需求下。

papers.title: AJ-Bench: Benchmarking Agent-as-a-Judge for Environment-Aware Evaluation
papers.abstract: Abstract Agent-as-a-Judge benchmark evaluates automated verification capabilities across multiple domains with comprehensive task assessment.  AI-generated summary As reinforcement learning continues to scale the training of large language model-based agents, reliably verifying agent behaviors in complex environments has become increasingly challenging. Existing approaches rely on rule-based verifiers or LLM-as-a-Judge models, which struggle to generalize beyond narrow domains. Agent-as-a-Judge addresses this limitation by actively interacting with environments and tools to acquire verifiable evidence, yet its capabilities remain underexplored.   We introduce a benchmark AJ-Bench to systematically evaluate Agent-as-a-Judge across three domains-search, data systems, and graphical user interfaces-comprising 155 tasks and 516 annotated trajectories. The benchmark comprehensively assesses judge agents' abilities in information acquisition, state verification, and process verification. Experiments demonstrate consistent performance gains over LLM-as-a-Judge baselines, while also revealing substantial open challenges in agent-based verification. Our data and code are available at https://aj-bench.github.io/.
affiliations: University of Science and Technology of China

ru.title: Агент-судья: активная верификация поведения через взаимодействие с окружением
ru.desc: В статье представлен бенчмарк AJ-Bench для оценки способности агентов на основе больших языковых моделей выступать в роли судей при проверке поведения других агентов. Авторы показывают, что подход Agent-as-a-Judge превосходит традиционные методы верификации на основе правил и LLM-as-a-Judge, активно взаимодействуя с окружением для сбора доказательств. Бенчмарк охватывает три домена (поиск, системы данных, графические интерфейсы) с 155 задачами и 516 аннотированными траекториями агентов. Исследование демонстрирует преимущества интерактивной верификации, но также выявляет существенные вызовы в проверке поведения агентов.

en.title: Empowering AI Agents with Robust Verification through AJ-Bench
en.desc: The paper introduces the Agent-as-a-Judge benchmark (AJ-Bench) to evaluate the verification capabilities of AI agents in various domains. It highlights the limitations of existing verification methods, such as rule-based systems and LLM-as-a-Judge models, which often fail to generalize effectively. AJ-Bench consists of 155 tasks across three domains, focusing on the agents' abilities to acquire information and verify states and processes. The results show that Agent-as-a-Judge outperforms traditional models, while also identifying significant challenges that remain in the field of agent-based verification.

zh.title: 代理作为评判者：提升自动化验证能力的基准
zh.desc: 本论文介绍了Agent-as-a-Judge基准，旨在评估自动化验证能力。随着强化学习的发展，基于大型语言模型的代理在复杂环境中的行为验证变得更加困难。现有方法依赖于基于规则的验证器或LLM-as-a-Judge模型，难以在广泛领域中推广。我们提出的AJ-Bench基准系统地评估了代理在信息获取、状态验证和过程验证等方面的能力，并展示了相较于传统模型的性能提升。

papers.title: SmartPhotoCrafter: Unified Reasoning, Generation and Optimization for Automatic Photographic Image Editing
papers.abstract: Abstract SmartPhotoCrafter automates photographic image editing by combining image quality comprehension with targeted enhancement, using a reasoning-to-generation approach that eliminates the need for explicit human instructions.  AI-generated summary Traditional photographic image editing typically requires users to possess sufficient aesthetic understanding to provide appropriate instructions for adjusting image quality and camera parameters. However, this paradigm relies on explicit human instruction of aesthetic intent, which is often ambiguous, incomplete, or inaccessible to non-expert users. In this work, we propose SmartPhotoCrafter, an automatic photographic image editing method which formulates image editing as a tightly coupled reasoning-to-generation process. The proposed model first performs image quality comprehension and identifies deficiencies by the Image Critic module, and then the Photographic Artist module realizes targeted edits to enhance image appeal, eliminating the need for explicit human instructions. A multi-stage training pipeline is adopted: (i) Foundation pretraining to establish basic aesthetic understanding and editing capabilities, (ii) Adaptation with reasoning-guided multi-edit supervision to incorporate rich semantic guidance, and (iii) Coordinated reasoning-to generation reinforcement learning to jointly optimize reasoning and generation. During training, SmartPhotoCrafter emphasizes photo-realistic image generation, while supporting both image restoration and retouching tasks with consistent adherence to color- and tone-related semantics. We also construct a stage-specific dataset, which progressively builds reasoning and controllable generation, effective cross-module collaboration, and ultimately high-quality photographic enhancement. Experiments demonstrate that SmartPhotoCrafter outperforms existing generative models on the task of automatic photographic enhancement, achieving photo-realistic results while exhibiting higher tonal sensitivity to retouching instructions. Project page: https://github.com/vivoCameraResearch/SmartPhotoCrafter.
affiliations: vivo BlueImage Lab, vivo Mobile Communication Co., Ltd.

ru.title: Интеллектуальное редактирование фотографий без явных инструкций
ru.desc: SmartPhotoCrafter — это система автоматического редактирования фотографий, которая объединяет оценку качества изображения с целевыми улучшениями, используя подход рассуждение-к-генерации. Модель состоит из двух основных компонентов: модуль Image Critic анализирует качество и выявляет недостатки, а модуль Photographic Artist выполняет целевые редактирования для повышения привлекательности. Обучение проводится в три этапа: предварительное обучение для получения базовых эстетических знаний, адаптация с многошаговым контролем рассуждений и обучение с подкреплением для совместной оптимизации. Система демонстрирует превосходные результаты в автоматическом улучшении фотографий, обеспечивая фотореалистичные результаты с высокой чувствительностью к тональным параметрам.

en.title: Automating Image Editing with SmartPhotoCrafter
en.desc: SmartPhotoCrafter is an innovative tool for automating photographic image editing by integrating image quality understanding with targeted enhancements. It uses a reasoning-to-generation approach, which means it can make decisions about how to improve an image without needing specific instructions from users. The system consists of two main components: the Image Critic, which assesses image quality, and the Photographic Artist, which applies enhancements based on that assessment. Through a multi-stage training process, SmartPhotoCrafter learns to generate photo-realistic images while effectively handling tasks like restoration and retouching, outperforming existing models in automatic photographic enhancement.

zh.title: 智能摄影编辑，自动化提升图像质量
zh.desc: SmartPhotoCrafter是一种自动化的摄影图像编辑方法，它结合了图像质量理解和针对性增强，采用推理到生成的方式，消除了对明确人类指令的需求。该模型通过图像评论模块识别图像缺陷，然后通过摄影艺术家模块进行有针对性的编辑，以提升图像的吸引力。训练过程中，SmartPhotoCrafter强调照片真实感的生成，同时支持图像恢复和修饰任务，确保色彩和色调的一致性。实验表明，SmartPhotoCrafter在自动摄影增强任务中优于现有生成模型，能够实现更高质量的图像效果。

papers.title: Understanding and Enforcing Weight Disentanglement in Task Arithmetic
papers.abstract: Abstract Task arithmetic lacks theoretical explanation for its success, but the proposed OrthoReg method addresses this by promoting weight disentanglement through enforced orthogonality in weight updates during fine-tuning.  AI-generated summary Task arithmetic provides an efficient, training-free way to edit pre-trained models, yet lacks a fundamental theoretical explanation for its success. The existing concept of ``weight disentanglement" describes the ideal outcome of non-interfering task composition but does not reveal its underlying cause. Crucially, what intrinsic properties of the pre-trained model (θ_0) or the task vectors (τ_t) enable this disentanglement remains underexplored. In this paper, we introduce Task-Feature Specialization (TFS), a model's ability to allocate distinct internal features to different tasks, as the fundamental principle. We first prove that TFS is a sufficient condition for weight disentanglement. More importantly, we find that TFS also gives rise to an observable geometric consequence: weight vector orthogonality. This positions TFS as the common cause for both the desired functional outcome (disentanglement) and a measurable geometric property (orthogonality). This relationship provides the key insight for our method: since the abstract TFS property is intractable to enforce directly, we can instead promote weight disentanglement by shaping its concrete geometric consequence, orthogonality. Therefore, we propose OrthoReg, a simple and effective regularization method that actively enforces an internal orthogonal structure on weight updates (ΔW) that constitute τ_t during fine-tuning. And we theoretically prove that OrthoReg promotes disentanglement. Extensive experiments demonstrate that OrthoReg consistently and significantly enhances the performance of various task arithmetic methods. Code is available at https://github.com/RL-MIND/OrthoReg{https://github.com/RL-MIND/OrthoReg}.
affiliations: State Key Laboratory for Novel Software Technology, Nanjing University, China

ru.title: Ортогональность весов как ключ к композиции задач
ru.desc: Работа изучает теоретические основы task arithmetic — метода редактирования предобученных моделей без переобучения. Авторы вводят концепцию Task-Feature Specialization (TFS), которая объясняет способность модели распределять разные внутренние признаки для разных задач. Они доказывают, что TFS является достаточным условием для разделения весов и приводит к геометрическому следствию — ортогональности векторов весов. На основе этого понимания предложен метод OrthoReg, регуляризация, которая принудительно вводит ортогональную структуру в обновления весов при fine-tuning.

en.title: Enhancing Model Fine-Tuning with OrthoReg: The Power of Orthogonality
en.desc: This paper introduces a new method called OrthoReg, which enhances the process of fine-tuning pre-trained models by promoting weight disentanglement through orthogonality. The authors explain that Task-Feature Specialization (TFS) is a key principle that allows models to allocate distinct features for different tasks, leading to better performance. They prove that TFS is linked to weight disentanglement and results in orthogonal weight vectors, which can be measured geometrically. By enforcing orthogonality in weight updates, OrthoReg effectively improves the performance of task arithmetic methods without requiring extensive training.

zh.title: 通过正交性促进权重解耦的创新方法
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种名为OrthoReg的方法，旨在通过在微调过程中强制权重更新的正交性来促进权重解耦。任务算术虽然提供了一种高效的方式来编辑预训练模型，但缺乏理论基础。我们引入了任务特征专业化（TFS）的概念，证明了它是权重解耦的充分条件，并且与权重向量的正交性有直接关系。通过OrthoReg方法，我们能够有效地增强各种任务算术方法的性能。

papers.title: Dual-View Training for Instruction-Following Information Retrieval
papers.abstract: Abstract A dual-view data synthesis approach using polarity reversal enhances retrieval systems' ability to follow instructions by training models to distinguish between topic-relevant and instruction-compliant documents.  AI-generated summary Instruction-following information retrieval (IF-IR) studies retrieval systems that must not only find documents relevant to a query, but also obey explicit user constraints such as required attributes, exclusions, or output preferences. However, most retrievers are trained primarily for semantic relevance and often fail to distinguish documents that match the topic from those that satisfy the instruction. We propose a dual-view data synthesis strategy based on polarity reversal: given a query, a document that is relevant under the instruction, and a hard negative that matches the query but violates the instruction, we prompt an LLM to generate a complementary instruction under which the two documents swap relevance labels. By presenting the same document pair under complementary instructions that invert their relevance labels, the training signal forces the retriever to reconsider the same candidate set through the instruction, rather than relying on fixed topical cues. On a 305M-parameter encoder, our method improves performance on the FollowIR benchmark by 45%, surpassing general-purpose embedding models of comparable or larger scale. Through head-to-head comparisons at matched data budgets, we further show that data diversity and instruction supervision play complementary roles: the former preserves general retrieval quality, while the latter improves instruction sensitivity. These results highlight the value of targeted data synthesis for building retrieval systems that are both broadly capable and instruction-aware.

ru.title: Инверсия инструкций для обучения поиска, осознающего ограничения пользователя
ru.desc: В статье предложен метод синтеза данных для улучшения систем информационного поиска, которые должны следовать явным инструкциям пользователя, а не только находить семантически релевантные документы. Авторы используют технику инверсии полярности: для одной пары документов генерируют дополнительные инструкции, при которых метки релевантности меняются местами, заставляя модель учитывать инструкции при принятии решений. Такой подход обучения на синтезированных данных позволяет кодировщику лучше различать документы, соответствующие теме, от документов, нарушающих ограничения пользователя. На бенчмарке FollowIR метод показал улучшение на 45%, демонстрируя эффективность целевого синтеза данных для обучения инструкционно-чувствительных систем поиска.

en.title: Enhancing Instruction-Following in Retrieval Systems with Dual-View Synthesis
en.desc: This paper introduces a dual-view data synthesis method that improves instruction-following in information retrieval systems. By using polarity reversal, the approach trains models to differentiate between documents that are relevant to a topic and those that comply with specific user instructions. The method involves generating complementary instructions that swap relevance labels for document pairs, enhancing the model's ability to process instructions effectively. The results show a significant performance boost on the FollowIR benchmark, demonstrating the importance of data diversity and instruction supervision in creating more capable retrieval systems.

zh.title: 双视角数据合成，提升检索系统的指令遵循能力
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种双视角数据合成方法，通过极性反转来增强检索系统遵循指令的能力。该方法训练模型区分与主题相关的文档和符合指令的文档。通过生成互补指令，文档的相关性标签得以交换，从而迫使检索器重新考虑候选集。实验结果表明，该方法在FollowIR基准测试中提高了45%的性能，显示了针对性数据合成在构建既具广泛能力又能遵循指令的检索系统中的重要性。

papers.title: Code-Switching Information Retrieval: Benchmarks, Analysis, and the Limits of Current Retrievers
papers.abstract: Abstract Code-switching poses significant challenges for information retrieval systems, revealing performance bottlenecks and embedding space divergences that current multilingual approaches cannot fully address.  AI-generated summary Code-switching is a pervasive linguistic phenomenon in global communication, yet modern information retrieval systems remain predominantly designed for, and evaluated within, monolingual contexts. To bridge this critical disconnect, we present a holistic study dedicated to code-switching IR. We introduce CSR-L (Code-Switching Retrieval benchmark-Lite), constructing a dataset via human annotation to capture the authentic naturalness of mixed-language queries. Our evaluation across statistical, dense, and late-interaction paradigms reveals that code-switching acts as a fundamental performance bottleneck, degrading the effectiveness of even robust multilingual models. We demonstrate that this failure stems from substantial divergence in the embedding space between pure and code-switched text. Scaling this investigation, we propose CS-MTEB, a comprehensive benchmark covering 11 diverse tasks, where we observe performance declines of up to 27%. Finally, we show that standard multilingual techniques like vocabulary expansion are insufficient to resolve these deficits completely. These findings underscore the fragility of current systems and establish code-switching as a crucial frontier for future IR optimization.

ru.title: Кодовые переключения - слабое звено современного поиска
ru.desc: В работе исследуется проблема кодовых переключений (смешивания языков) в системах поиска информации. Авторы создали эталонный набор данных CSR-L с естественными запросами на смешанных языках и обнаружили, что кодовые переключения значительно снижают эффективность даже мощных многоязычных моделей вплоть до 27%. Причина проблемы заключается в существенной расходимости представлений в пространстве эмбеддингов между чистыми и смешанными текстами. Стандартные методы адаптации многоязычных систем, такие как расширение словаря, оказываются недостаточными для решения этой проблемы.

en.title: Bridging the Gap: Tackling Code-Switching in Information Retrieval
en.desc: This paper addresses the challenges that code-switching presents to information retrieval (IR) systems, which are typically designed for monolingual use. The authors introduce CSR-L, a new dataset created through human annotation to better reflect mixed-language queries. Their research shows that code-switching significantly hampers the performance of multilingual models due to differences in the embedding spaces of pure and code-switched text. They also propose CS-MTEB, a benchmark for evaluating various tasks, revealing that existing multilingual techniques are inadequate for overcoming the performance issues caused by code-switching.

zh.title: 代码切换：信息检索的新挑战
zh.desc: 代码切换是全球交流中常见的语言现象，但现代信息检索系统主要针对单语环境设计，导致其在处理代码切换时表现不佳。本文提出了CSR-L（代码切换检索基准-Lite），通过人工标注构建数据集，以捕捉混合语言查询的自然性。研究表明，代码切换显著降低了多语言模型的效果，尤其是在嵌入空间的差异上。我们还提出了CS-MTEB基准，涵盖11个不同任务，发现性能下降可达27%，强调了代码切换在信息检索优化中的重要性。

papers.title: CityRAG: Stepping Into a City via Spatially-Grounded Video Generation
papers.abstract: Abstract CityRAG generates long-term, physically grounded video sequences that maintain environmental consistency and support complex navigation through real-world geography using geo-registered data as context.  AI-generated summary We address the problem of generating a 3D-consistent, navigable environment that is spatially grounded: a simulation of a real location. Existing video generative models can produce a plausible sequence that is consistent with a text (T2V) or image (I2V) prompt. However, the capability to reconstruct the real world under arbitrary weather conditions and dynamic object configurations is essential for downstream applications including autonomous driving and robotics simulation. To this end, we present CityRAG, a video generative model that leverages large corpora of geo-registered data as context to ground generation to the physical scene, while maintaining learned priors for complex motion and appearance changes. CityRAG relies on temporally unaligned training data, which teaches the model to semantically disentangle the underlying scene from its transient attributes. Our experiments demonstrate that CityRAG can generate coherent minutes-long, physically grounded video sequences, maintain weather and lighting conditions over thousands of frames, achieve loop closure, and navigate complex trajectories to reconstruct real-world geography.

ru.title: География в цифре: видео реального мира из геопривязанных данных
ru.desc: CityRAG — это генеративная модель видео, которая создаёт длинные видеопоследовательности, привязанные к реальным географическим местам с помощью георегистрированных данных. Модель использует технику Retrieval-Augmented Generation для контекстуализации генерации, позволяя поддерживать физическую консистентность окружающей среды при различных условиях освещения и погоды. Ключевая инновация заключается в способности работать с несогласованными по времени обучающими данными, что позволяет модели семантически разделить статичную сцену и её изменяющиеся атрибуты. Система демонстрирует возможность генерировать физически реалистичные видео протяжённостью в несколько минут с замыканием циклов и навигацией по сложным траекториям для реконструкции реальной географии.

en.title: Generating Realistic, Navigable Video Sequences with CityRAG
en.desc: CityRAG is a video generative model designed to create long, realistic video sequences that accurately reflect real-world environments. It uses geo-registered data to ensure that the generated videos are physically grounded and maintain consistency in weather and lighting conditions. Unlike traditional models that rely on aligned data, CityRAG learns to separate the stable aspects of a scene from its changing elements, allowing for complex navigation and realistic simulations. This capability is particularly useful for applications in autonomous driving and robotics, where understanding the environment is crucial.

zh.title: 生成真实世界的视频序列
zh.desc: CityRAG是一种视频生成模型，能够生成长期的、与物理环境一致的视频序列。这种模型利用地理注册数据作为上下文，确保生成的视频与真实场景相符。它可以在不同的天气条件和动态物体配置下重建现实世界，适用于自动驾驶和机器人模拟等应用。通过对时间未对齐的训练数据进行学习，CityRAG能够有效地分离场景的基本特征与瞬态属性。

papers.title: Speculative Decoding for Autoregressive Video Generation
papers.abstract: Abstract Speculative decoding is adapted to autoregressive video diffusion through a quality-based routing mechanism that maintains high visual quality while achieving significant speedup.  AI-generated summary Autoregressive video diffusion is emerging as a promising paradigm for streaming video synthesis, with step distillation serving as the primary means of accelerating inference. Whether speculative decoding, the dominant acceleration strategy for large language models, can be effectively adapted to autoregressive video generation remains an open question, because video blocks are continuous spatiotemporal tensors with no token-level distribution for exact rejection sampling. We introduce SDVG, which brings speculative decoding to block-based autoregressive video diffusion by replacing token verification with an image-quality router. A 1.3B drafter proposes candidate blocks via four denoising steps; each block is VAE-decoded and scored by ImageReward using worst-frame aggregation--taking the minimum per-frame reward to catch single-frame artifacts that averaging would mask. Blocks scoring above a fixed threshold tau are accepted into the 14B target's KV cache; the rest are regenerated by the target. Two additional design choices prove critical: the first block is always force-rejected to anchor scene composition, and tau serves as a single knob that traces a smooth quality-speed Pareto frontier. On 1003 MovieGenVideoBench prompts (832x480), SDVG retains 98.1% of target-only VisionReward quality (0.0773 vs. 0.0788) at a 1.59x speedup with tau=-0.7, and reaches 2.09x at 95.7% quality retention--while consistently outperforming draft-only generation by over +17%. The framework is training-free, requires no architectural changes, and can be seamlessly integrated into existing autoregressive video generation pipelines.

ru.title: Быстрое видео без потери качества через спекулятивное декодирование
ru.desc: В работе представлен метод адаптации спекулятивного декодирования для автрегрессивной видеодиффузии, которая позволяет ускорить генерацию видео без заметной потери качества. Вместо точной проверки токенов, используется маршрутизатор на основе оценки качества изображений, где небольшая модель-драфтер предлагает кандидатные блоки видео, а их качество оценивается с помощью ImageReward. Каждый видеоблок декодируется через VAE и оценивается по худшему кадру в последовательности, чтобы поймать артефакты, которые усреднение могло бы скрыть. Метод достигает ускорения в 1,59x при сохранении 98,1% качества целевой модели и может быть интегрирован в существующие системы видеогенерации без переобучения.

en.title: Speed Meets Quality in Video Generation with SDVG
en.desc: This paper presents SDVG, a novel approach that applies speculative decoding to autoregressive video diffusion, enhancing the speed of video generation while maintaining high visual quality. The method utilizes a quality-based routing mechanism that evaluates candidate video blocks based on their visual fidelity, allowing for efficient selection and rejection of frames. By employing a unique scoring system that focuses on the worst-frame quality, SDVG ensures that artifacts are minimized in the final output. The results demonstrate significant speed improvements without compromising quality, making it a valuable addition to existing video generation frameworks.

zh.title: 推测解码加速自回归视频生成
zh.desc: 本论文提出了一种名为SDVG的框架，将推测解码技术应用于自回归视频扩散。该方法通过质量路由机制，确保生成视频的高视觉质量，同时显著提高生成速度。SDVG使用图像质量评分来筛选视频块，并通过四个去噪步骤生成候选块，确保最终输出的质量。实验结果表明，SDVG在保持高质量的同时，速度提升可达2.09倍，且无需对现有架构进行修改。

papers.title: Target-Oriented Pretraining Data Selection via Neuron-Activated Graph
papers.abstract: Abstract A novel target-oriented language model pretraining framework uses neuron activation graphs to select informative data without additional training, demonstrating superior performance across multiple benchmarks.  AI-generated summary Everyday tasks come with a target, and pretraining models around this target is what turns them into experts. In this paper, we study target-oriented language model (LM) pretraining by introducing Neuron-Activated Graph Ranking (NAG-based Ranking), a training-free and interpretable framework for target pretraining data selection. Rather than using black-box representations, our approach directly characterizes each target input by a sparse set of high-impact neurons in any off-the-shelf LLMs. Concretely, we quantify neuron impact and select the most influential neurons across layers into a compact Neuron-Activated Graph (NAG), and rank candidate data by NAG similarity to target examples. We conduct experiments across six benchmarks, where our NAG-based Ranking improves target-oriented pretraining by 4.9% on average over random sampling, and also outperforms state-of-the-art baselines by 5.3% accuracy on HellaSwag. It also remains effective under a more applicable multi-target setting, where our best setup surpasses two baselines by 1.1% and 4.1%, respectively. Furthermore, we provide a comprehensive analysis on why and how our NAG works, e.g., deactivating NAG-selected neurons (only 0.12% of all) causes a 23.5% performance collapse, and restricting NAG to the final layer incurs a 4.1% average drop, indicating that NAG captures a sparse "functional backbone" for learning target features. We release the code at https://github.com/asillycat/NAG.

ru.title: Целевая подготовка языковых моделей с помощью нейронных графов
ru.desc: В статье представлена новая методика предварительного обучения языковых моделей, ориентированная на конкретные цели, с использованием графов активации нейронов. Эта методика позволяет выбирать информативные данные без дополнительного обучения, что улучшает производительность моделей. Авторы используют графы активации нейронов для оценки влияния нейронов и выбора наиболее значимых из них. Эксперименты показывают, что такой подход улучшает точность моделей на различных тестах и превосходит современные методы.

en.title: Targeted Pretraining with Neuron Activation Insights
en.desc: This paper presents a new framework for pretraining language models that focuses on specific targets using Neuron-Activated Graphs (NAG). Instead of traditional methods, it selects data based on the activation of influential neurons in existing language models, making the process interpretable and efficient. The authors demonstrate that their approach significantly improves performance on various benchmarks, outperforming random sampling and existing state-of-the-art methods. Additionally, they analyze the importance of the selected neurons, showing that their method captures essential features for effective target-oriented learning.

zh.title: 目标导向预训练：用神经元激活图提升性能
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种新颖的目标导向语言模型预训练框架，利用神经元激活图选择有用的数据，而无需额外训练。我们引入了基于神经元激活图的排名方法（NAG-based Ranking），该方法可以直观地选择目标预训练数据。通过量化神经元的影响力，我们构建了一个紧凑的神经元激活图，并根据其与目标示例的相似性对候选数据进行排名。实验结果表明，NAG-based Ranking在多个基准测试中显著提高了目标导向预训练的性能。

papers.title: UniMesh: Unifying 3D Mesh Understanding and Generation
papers.abstract: Abstract UniMesh presents a unified framework that combines 3D generation and understanding tasks through novel components including a Mesh Head, Chain of Mesh for iterative editing, and a self-reflection mechanism for error correction.  AI-generated summary Recent advances in 3D vision have led to specialized models for either 3D understanding (e.g., shape classification, segmentation, reconstruction) or 3D generation (e.g., synthesis, completion, and editing). However, these tasks are often tackled in isolation, resulting in fragmented architectures and representations that hinder knowledge transfer and holistic scene modeling. To address these challenges, we propose UniMesh, a unified framework that jointly learns 3D generation and understanding within a single architecture. First, we introduce a novel Mesh Head that acts as a cross model interface, bridging diffusion based image generation with implicit shape decoders. Second, we develop Chain of Mesh (CoM), a geometric instantiation of iterative reasoning that enables user driven semantic mesh editing through a closed loop latent, prompting, and re generation cycle. Third, we incorporate a self reflection mechanism based on an Actor Evaluator Self reflection triad to diagnose and correct failures in high level tasks like 3D captioning. Experimental results demonstrate that UniMesh not only achieves competitive performance on standard benchmarks but also unlocks novel capabilities in iterative editing and mutual enhancement between generation and understanding. Code: https://github.com/AIGeeksGroup/UniMesh. Website: https://aigeeksgroup.github.io/UniMesh.

ru.title: Единая архитектура для генерации и понимания 3D-сеток с итеративным редактированием
ru.desc: UniMesh представляет унифицированный фреймворк, который объединяет задачи генерации и понимания 3D-объектов в единую архитектуру. Ключевой компонент Mesh Head служит интерфейсом между диффузионными моделями генерации и неявными декодерами форм. Chain of Mesh реализует итеративное рассуждение для интерактивного редактирования сеток через замкнутый цикл в скрытом пространстве. Механизм самоконтроля на основе триады Actor-Evaluator-Self reflection позволяет диагностировать и исправлять ошибки в высокоуровневых задачах вроде 3D-описания.

en.title: UniMesh: Bridging 3D Generation and Understanding for Enhanced Modeling
en.desc: UniMesh is a novel framework that integrates 3D generation and understanding tasks into a single architecture, enhancing the synergy between these processes. It introduces a Mesh Head that connects image generation with shape decoding, facilitating better interaction between models. The Chain of Mesh (CoM) allows for iterative editing of 3D meshes, enabling users to refine their designs through a feedback loop. Additionally, a self-reflection mechanism helps identify and correct errors in complex tasks like 3D captioning, leading to improved performance and new capabilities in 3D modeling.

zh.title: 统一3D生成与理解的创新框架
zh.desc: UniMesh是一个统一的框架，结合了3D生成和理解任务。它引入了Mesh Head、Mesh链和自我反思机制等新组件，以实现迭代编辑和错误修正。通过这种方式，UniMesh能够在单一架构中共同学习3D生成和理解，克服了以往任务孤立处理带来的问题。实验结果表明，UniMesh在标准基准测试中表现出色，并在迭代编辑和生成与理解之间的相互增强方面展现了新能力。

papers.title: RDP LoRA: Geometry-Driven Identification for Parameter-Efficient Adaptation in Large Language Models
papers.abstract: Abstract Using geometric trajectory analysis with the Ramer-Douglas-Peucker algorithm to select optimal layers for parameter-efficient fine-tuning of large language models, achieving better performance than full or random layer selection.  AI-generated summary Fine-tuning Large Language Models (LLMs) remains structurally uncertain despite parameter-efficient methods such as Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA), as the layer-specific roles of internal representations are poorly understood, leading to heuristic decisions about where adaptation should be applied. We model the evolution of hidden states as a high-dimensional geometric trajectory and propose using the Ramer-Douglas-Peucker (RDP) algorithm, a parameter-free and training-free polygon simplification method that preserves global structural transitions while eliminating locally redundant changes, to identify critical breakpoints along the representation path. Crucially, we use these geometric pivots not merely for analysis, but as a direct decision signal for determining which layers should be adapted during parameter-efficient fine-tuning. By integrating this geometry-aware layer selection strategy into LoRA fine-tuning of Qwen3-8B-Base, we achieve superior performance on MMLU-Math using only 13 RDP-selected layers (81.67%), significantly outperforming both full 36-layer adaptation (79.32%) and random 13-layer selection (75.56%), as well as the baseline Qwen3-8B-Base model (74.25%). These results demonstrate that leveraging the intrinsic geometry of representation trajectories provides a robust, interpretable, and training-free signal for optimizing layer selection during model adaptation.

ru.title: Геометрия скрытых состояний как навигатор для умного выбора слоёв
ru.desc: В работе предлагается использовать геометрический анализ траекторий скрытых состояний нейронной сети для выбора оптимальных слоёв при параметрически эффективной дообучении больших языковых моделей. Авторы применяют алгоритм Рамера-Дугласа-Пёккера, который обычно используется для упрощения полигонов, чтобы выявить критические переходы в представлениях модели и определить, какие именно слои нужно адаптировать. При дообучении модели Qwen3-8B-Base с использованием LoRA на только 13 выбранных слоях они достигают точности 81.67% на MMLU-Math, превосходя как полное дообучение всех 36 слоёв (79.32%), так и случайный выбор. Этот подход демонстрирует, что анализ внутренней геометрии представлений позволяет находить оптимальные слои для адаптации без дополнительного обучения.

en.title: Optimize Layer Selection with Geometry!
en.desc: This paper introduces a novel approach for fine-tuning large language models (LLMs) by utilizing geometric trajectory analysis. It employs the Ramer-Douglas-Peucker (RDP) algorithm to identify key layers for adaptation, enhancing performance while minimizing the number of layers adjusted. The method focuses on the geometric structure of hidden states, allowing for a more informed selection of layers compared to traditional random or full-layer adaptation strategies. Results show that this geometry-aware approach significantly improves performance on specific tasks, demonstrating its effectiveness in optimizing parameter-efficient fine-tuning.

zh.title: 几何轨迹优化大语言模型微调层选择
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种基于几何轨迹分析的方法，利用Ramer-Douglas-Peucker算法选择适合的层，以实现大语言模型的高效微调。通过对隐藏状态的演变进行建模，我们能够识别出关键的断点，从而优化层的选择。与传统的全层或随机层选择相比，这种方法在MMLU-Math任务上表现更佳，仅使用13个选定层就达到了81.67%的准确率。研究表明，利用表示轨迹的内在几何特性，可以为模型适应过程中的层选择提供稳健且可解释的信号。

papers.title: LoopCTR: Unlocking the Loop Scaling Power for Click-Through Rate Prediction
papers.abstract: Abstract LoopCTR introduces a loop scaling paradigm for CTR models that increases training computation through recursive layer reuse while maintaining efficient inference, achieving state-of-the-art performance with enhanced adaptive inference potential.  AI-generated summary Scaling Transformer-based click-through rate (CTR) models by stacking more parameters brings growing computational and storage overhead, creating a widening gap between scaling ambitions and the stringent industrial deployment constraints. We propose LoopCTR, which introduces a loop scaling paradigm that increases training-time computation through recursive reuse of shared model layers, decoupling computation from parameter growth. LoopCTR adopts a sandwich architecture enhanced with Hyper-Connected Residuals and Mixture-of-Experts, and employs process supervision at every loop depth to encode multi-loop benefits into the shared parameters. This enables a train-multi-loop, infer-zero-loop strategy where a single forward pass without any loop already outperforms all baselines. Experiments on three public benchmarks and one industrial dataset demonstrate state-of-the-art performance. Oracle analysis further reveals 0.02--0.04 AUC of untapped headroom, with models trained with fewer loops exhibiting higher oracle ceilings, pointing to a promising frontier for adaptive inference.
affiliations: Gaoling School of Artificial Intelligence, Renmin University of China

ru.title: Эффективное масштабирование через рекурсивное переиспользование параметров
ru.desc: LoopCTR предлагает новую парадигму масштабирования для моделей предсказания кликов, которая увеличивает вычисления во время обучения путём рекурсивного переиспользования слоёв нейронной сети, без увеличения количества параметров. Архитектура основана на сэндвич-структуре с гиперсвязными остаточными связями и механизмом смеси экспертов, с применением процессного надзора на каждом уровне рекурсии. Ключевой инновацией является стратегия «обучение с множественными циклами, инференс без циклов», где одного прямого прохода хватает для превосходства над базовыми моделями. Модель достигает лучших результатов на публичных и промышленных датасетах, при этом оставляя потенциал для адаптивного инференса.

en.title: LoopCTR: Efficient Scaling for Superior CTR Performance
en.desc: LoopCTR presents a novel approach to enhance click-through rate (CTR) models by implementing a loop scaling paradigm. This method allows for increased computational efficiency during training by reusing model layers, which helps to reduce the need for additional parameters. The architecture incorporates Hyper-Connected Residuals and Mixture-of-Experts, facilitating effective multi-loop training while maintaining a streamlined inference process. Experimental results show that LoopCTR achieves superior performance on various benchmarks, indicating its potential for adaptive inference in real-world applications.

zh.title: 循环缩放，提升CTR模型性能
zh.desc: LoopCTR提出了一种循环缩放范式，用于点击率（CTR）模型，通过递归重用共享模型层来增加训练计算，同时保持高效的推理性能。这种方法解耦了计算与参数增长，采用了增强的三明治架构，结合超连接残差和专家混合技术。在每个循环深度上进行过程监督，使得共享参数能够编码多循环的好处。实验结果表明，LoopCTR在多个公共基准和一个工业数据集上实现了最先进的性能，展示了适应性推理的潜力。

papers.title: HP-Edit: A Human-Preference Post-Training Framework for Image Editing
papers.abstract: Abstract A post-training framework called HP-Edit is introduced to align image editing models with human preferences using a novel automatic evaluator and a real-world dataset, improving editing quality through reinforcement learning techniques.  AI-generated summary Common image editing tasks typically adopt powerful generative diffusion models as the leading paradigm for real-world content editing. Meanwhile, although reinforcement learning (RL) methods such as Diffusion-DPO and Flow-GRPO have further improved generation quality, efficiently applying Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (RLHF) to diffusion-based editing remains largely unexplored, due to a lack of scalable human-preference datasets and frameworks tailored to diverse editing needs. To fill this gap, we propose HP-Edit, a post-training framework for Human Preference-aligned Editing, and introduce RealPref-50K, a real-world dataset across eight common tasks and balancing common object editing. Specifically, HP-Edit leverages a small amount of human-preference scoring data and a pretrained visual large language model (VLM) to develop HP-Scorer--an automatic, human preference-aligned evaluator. We then use HP-Scorer both to efficiently build a scalable preference dataset and to serve as the reward function for post-training the editing model. We also introduce RealPref-Bench, a benchmark for evaluating real-world editing performance. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our approach significantly enhances models such as Qwen-Image-Edit-2509, aligning their outputs more closely with human preference.

ru.title: Выравнивание моделей редактирования с человеческими предпочтениями через обучение с подкреплением
ru.desc: Представлена HP-Edit — фреймворк постобучения для выравнивания моделей редактирования изображений с предпочтениями человека. Авторы разработали HP-Scorer, автоматический оценщик на основе предобученной визуальной большой языковой модели, который использует небольшой объём данных с человеческими оценками. Для обучения с подкреплением на основе обратной связи от человека (RLHF) был создан датасет RealPref-50K, охватывающий восемь типичных задач редактирования. Экспериментальные результаты показывают значительное улучшение качества редактирования и лучшее согласование выходных результатов с человеческими предпочтениями.

en.title: Aligning Image Editing with Human Preferences
en.desc: The paper presents HP-Edit, a framework designed to improve image editing models by aligning them with human preferences. It introduces RealPref-50K, a dataset that includes diverse editing tasks and human-preference scores, which helps in training the models more effectively. By utilizing reinforcement learning techniques and a pretrained visual large language model, HP-Edit creates an automatic evaluator called HP-Scorer to assess and enhance editing quality. The results show that HP-Edit significantly improves the alignment of model outputs with human preferences, demonstrating its effectiveness in real-world applications.

zh.title: HP-Edit：让图像编辑更符合人类偏好
zh.desc: 本文介绍了一种名为HP-Edit的后训练框架，旨在通过自动评估器和真实世界数据集，使图像编辑模型与人类偏好对齐。该框架利用少量人类偏好评分数据和预训练的视觉大语言模型（VLM），开发了HP-Scorer，一个自动化的评估工具。HP-Edit通过强化学习技术提高编辑质量，并引入了RealPref-50K数据集，涵盖八种常见任务，平衡了常见对象的编辑需求。实验结果表明，该方法显著提升了模型的输出质量，使其更符合人类的偏好。

papers.title: MM-JudgeBias: A Benchmark for Evaluating Compositional Biases in MLLM-as-a-Judge
papers.abstract: Abstract Research identifies systematic biases in multimodal large language models used as automatic evaluators, revealing reliability issues and proposing a benchmark for measuring compositional bias through controlled perturbations and specific metrics.  AI-generated summary Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) have been increasingly used as automatic evaluators-a paradigm known as MLLM-as-a-Judge. However, their reliability and vulnerabilities to biases remain underexplored. We find that many MLLM judges fail to reliably integrate key visual or textual cues, yielding unreliable evaluations when evidence is missing or mismatched, and exhibiting instability under semantically irrelevant perturbations. To address this, we systematically define Compositional Bias in MLLM-as-a-Judge systems and introduce MM-JudgeBias, a benchmark for evaluating it. MM-JudgeBias introduces controlled perturbations across Query, Image, and Response, and evaluates model behavior via two complementary metrics: Bias-Deviation (BD) for sensitivity and Bias-Conformity (BC) for stability. Our dataset of over 1,800 curated and refined multimodal samples, drawn from 29 source benchmarks, enables a fine-grained diagnosis of nine bias types across diverse tasks and domains. Experiments on 26 state-of-the-art MLLMs reveal systematic modality neglect and asymmetric evaluation tendencies, underscoring the need for more reliable judges.

ru.title: Выявление и измерение композиционных смещений в мультимодальных оценивающих моделях
ru.desc: Исследование выявляет систематические смещения в мультимодальных больших языковых моделях, используемых как автоматические оценивающие системы. Авторы обнаружили, что многие такие модели неправильно интегрируют визуальные и текстовые признаки, что приводит к ненадёжным оценкам при отсутствии или несоответствии доказательств. Для решения этой проблемы предложена новая метрика композиционного смещения и benchmark MM-JudgeBias с контролируемыми возмущениями входных данных. Экспериментальное тестирование 26 современных мультимодальных моделей показало систематическое игнорирование модальностей и асимметричные тенденции в оценивании.

en.title: Unveiling Biases in Multimodal Evaluators: The Need for Reliable Judging
en.desc: This paper investigates the biases present in multimodal large language models (MLLMs) when they are used as automatic evaluators. It highlights that these models often struggle to effectively combine visual and textual information, leading to inconsistent evaluations, especially when the input data is altered. The authors introduce a new benchmark called MM-JudgeBias, which measures compositional bias through controlled changes in queries, images, and responses, using two metrics: Bias-Deviation and Bias-Conformity. The study reveals significant reliability issues in current MLLM evaluators and emphasizes the importance of addressing these biases for improved model performance.

zh.title: 揭示多模态评估者的偏差问题
zh.desc: 本研究揭示了多模态大型语言模型（MLLMs）作为自动评估者时存在的系统性偏差，指出了其可靠性问题。我们发现，许多MLLM评估者在整合关键的视觉或文本线索时表现不稳定，导致在证据缺失或不匹配时的评估不可靠。为了解决这个问题，我们系统性地定义了MLLM作为评估者系统中的组合偏差，并引入了MM-JudgeBias基准来进行评估。该基准通过控制查询、图像和响应的扰动，并使用偏差偏离度（BD）和偏差一致性（BC）两个互补指标来评估模型行为。

papers.title: UDM-GRPO: Stable and Efficient Group Relative Policy Optimization for Uniform Discrete Diffusion Models
papers.abstract: Abstract Uniform Discrete Diffusion Model integrated with reinforcement learning through novel optimization strategies achieves state-of-the-art performance in text-to-image tasks and OCR benchmarks.  AI-generated summary Uniform Discrete Diffusion Model (UDM) has recently emerged as a promising paradigm for discrete generative modeling; however, its integration with reinforcement learning remains largely unexplored. We observe that naively applying GRPO to UDM leads to training instability and marginal performance gains. To address this, we propose \Ours, the first framework to integrate UDM with RL. Our method is guided by two key insights: (i) treating the final clean sample as the action provides more accurate and stable optimization signals; and (ii) reconstructing trajectories via the diffusion forward process better aligns probability paths with the pretraining distribution. Additionally, we introduce two strategies, Reduced-Step and CFG-Free, to further improve training efficiency. \Ours significantly improves base model performance across multiple T2I tasks. Notably, GenEval accuracy improves from 69% to 96% and PickScore increases from 20.46 to 23.81, achieving state-of-the-art performance in both continuous and discrete settings. On the OCR benchmark, accuracy rises from 8% to 57%, further validating the generalization ability of our method. Code is available at https://github.com/Yovecent/UDM-GRPO{https://github.com/Yovecent/UDM-GRPO}.
affiliations: Beijing University of Academy of Artificial Intelligence. Posts and Telecommunications

ru.title: Диффузные модели встречают обучение с подкреплением: путь к идеальной генерации
ru.desc: В статье предложен первый фреймворк для интеграции Uniform Discrete Diffusion Model с обучением с подкреплением, решающий проблему нестабильности при прямом применении GRPO. Ключевая идея заключается в том, чтобы рассматривать финальный чистый образец как действие и реконструировать траектории через процесс прямой диффузии для более стабильных сигналов оптимизации. Авторы вводят две стратегии (Reduced-Step и CFG-Free) для повышения эффективности обучения. Метод достигает state-of-the-art результатов на задачах синтеза изображений из текста и распознавания текста с значительным улучшением метрик.

en.title: Revolutionizing Text-to-Image Generation with Reinforcement Learning
en.desc: The paper presents a novel framework that combines the Uniform Discrete Diffusion Model (UDM) with reinforcement learning (RL) to enhance performance in text-to-image (T2I) tasks and optical character recognition (OCR). It identifies challenges in training stability when using traditional methods like GRPO with UDM and proposes solutions to improve optimization signals and align probability paths. The authors introduce two innovative strategies, Reduced-Step and CFG-Free, to boost training efficiency and model performance. As a result, their approach achieves significant accuracy improvements, setting new benchmarks in both T2I and OCR tasks.

zh.title: 将均匀离散扩散模型与强化学习结合的创新方法
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种新的框架，将均匀离散扩散模型（UDM）与强化学习（RL）相结合，以提高文本到图像生成任务和光学字符识别（OCR）基准的性能。我们发现，简单地将GRPO应用于UDM会导致训练不稳定和性能提升有限。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了	extbackslash Ours框架，通过将最终的干净样本视为动作，提供更准确和稳定的优化信号，并通过扩散前向过程重建轨迹，更好地对齐概率路径与预训练分布。此外，我们引入了两种策略，Reduced-Step和CFG-Free，以进一步提高训练效率。

papers.title: SkillLearnBench: Benchmarking Continual Learning Methods for Agent Skill Generation on Real-World Tasks
papers.abstract: Abstract Continual skill learning methods for LLM agents show mixed performance across diverse tasks, with improvements dependent on task structure and feedback mechanisms rather than model scaling.  AI-generated summary Skills have become the de facto way to enable LLM agents to perform complex real-world tasks with customized instructions, workflows, and tools, but how to learn them automatically and effectively remains unclear. We introduce SkillLearnBench, the first benchmark for evaluating continual skill learning methods, comprising 20 verified, skill-dependent tasks across 15 sub-domains derived from a real-world skill taxonomy , evaluated at three levels: skill quality, execution trajectory, and task outcome. Using this benchmark, we evaluate recent continual learning techniques, those leveraging one-shot, self/teacher feedback, and skill creator to generate skills from agent experiences. We find that all continual learning methods improve over the no-skill baseline, yet consistent gains remain elusive: no method leads across all tasks and LLMs, and scaling to stronger LLMs does not reliably help. Continual learning improves tasks with clear, reusable workflows but struggles on open-ended tasks, and using stronger LLM backbones does not consistently produce better skills. Our analysis also revealed that multiple iterations in continual learning facilitate genuine improvement via external feedback, whereas self-feedback alone induces recursive drift. Our data and code are open-source at https://github.com/cxcscmu/SkillLearnBench to enable further studies of automatic skill generation and continual learning techniques.

ru.title: Масштабирование модели — не панацея: качество навыков зависит от обратной связи, а не от размера
ru.desc: В статье представлен SkillLearnBench — первый бенчмарк для оценки методов непрерывного обучения навыкам у агентов на основе LLM. Авторы оценивают различные техники непрерывного обучения, включая one-shot обучение, самообратную связь и внешнюю обратную связь, на 20 задачах из реальной таксономии навыков.
